clusters.failureShape: When a modal, dialog, panel, or menu overlay opens, keyboard focus stays on the trigger instead of moving into the new content, so screen-reader and keyboard users don't know it opened and tab through the background page first.
clusters.fix: On open, move focus to the dialog container (or its first focusable element / heading) and trap focus inside it. On close, return focus to the trigger.

clusters.failureShape: After a navigation or step change (detail page, OTP/verification screen, multi-step form), focus and the screen-reader virtual cursor stay on the old DOM, so the user never reaches — or even hears — the new content.
clusters.fix: On each navigation or step change, move focus to the new view's heading or first control so assistive tech follows the transition. Announce the new page title.

clusters.failureShape: An in-page anchor or scroll-to-section link scrolls the page visually but doesn't move focus to the target section, so screen-reader users have to hunt for the content they jumped to.
clusters.fix: After activating an in-page link, move focus to the target section (give it tabindex="-1") so the reading position follows the jump.

clusters.failureShape: When a temporary region (search, modal) closes, focus isn't returned to the control that opened it, dropping the keyboard user at an unexpected place.
clusters.fix: On close, programmatically return focus to the triggering element so the keyboard user resumes where they left off.

clusters.failureShape: Visually hidden responsive variants of an element (duplicate CTAs, responsive H1s) keep tabindex="0" and remain in the tab order, so keyboard users hit the same control several times.
clusters.fix: Remove hidden duplicates from the tab order: drop tabindex on hidden variants and mark them aria-hidden="true" (or render only the active variant).
